---
title: Service templates
description: One-click catalog services — what they are, how to deploy them, and how each template is wired.
---

import { Card, CardGrid, Aside, LinkCard } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

A **template** is a prebuilt, one-click service — or a whole stack of services — that you pick from the
catalog in the **Create Service** flow. Instead of configuring images, ports, environment, and secrets
by hand, you choose a template, fill in a couple of inputs, and the platform creates the wired-up
services for you. Each one is an ordinary service afterwards: a Kubernetes Deployment behind a Service
and (optionally) an Ingress.

## How templates work

- **Catalog** — templates are served from a catalog the console reads, refreshed out-of-band, so new or
  updated templates appear without a platform upgrade.
- **Instantiation** — picking a template creates its services through the **same path as a manual
  service**. There is no parallel runtime; a templated service deploys, scales, and rolls back like any
  other.
- **Generated secrets** — templates declare secrets that are generated **per instance**: random
  passwords, and (for Supabase) HS256-signed JWT keys derived from a generated `jwt_secret`. You never
  type these.
- **Cross-service wiring** — services reference each other by their internal host with
  `{{ services.<name>.host }}`, resolved at instantiation (in any order, cycles included). The
  **primary** service is the public entrypoint and gets the default domain.
- **Inputs** — the few values you supply, e.g. a public domain.

## Deploying a template

1. Open an environment and click **Create Service**.
2. Pick a template from the catalog (top of the service-type picker).
3. Fill in the inputs (for example, a domain), then create.
4. The platform creates the services. Deploy them like any other service.

<Aside type="note">
	Generated secrets (database passwords, API keys) are encrypted at rest and injected into the containers as Kubernetes Secrets — you don't manage
	them by hand.
</Aside>
